summary: PR #2615 attempted to link `src/melee/gm/gmopening.c` by adjusting GALE01 split/symbol metadata and reshaping gmopening data declarations. The author stated that all code matched in objdiff, but marking the file as `Matching` still caused a data mismatch.

key_files.role: Main source file being linked; data declarations were reordered/split, inline format strings replaced pointer-offset uses, force-active float storage and sdata2 ordering helper were added.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gmopening.c removes static struct gm_803DBF78 containing floats and strings
raw_diff: gmopening.c adds address-commented Vec3, BSS, SBSS, and data declarations near file top
raw_diff: gmopening.c adds sdata2_order and force_active unused_floats
raw_diff: fn_801A9FCC now uses inline sprintf format strings

key_files.role: Split metadata changed to move BSS range ownership from gm_1A4C into gmopening.
Symbol metadata changed around gmopening data at 0x803DBF78.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_803DBF78 size changes from 0x3C global to 0x24 local data:float
raw_diff: adds lbl_803DBF9C and lbl_803DBFA8 as 0xC string data labels

what_changed.text: Changed `gm_803DBF78` from one local C struct bundling nine floats plus two strings into separate metadata: a local 0x24 float object and two string labels in symbols.txt.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: symbols.txt lbl_803DBF9C and lbl_803DBFA8 additions
raw_diff: gmopening.c removes struct containing f32 data[9], x24 string, x30 string

what_changed.text: Replaced pointer arithmetic into the former `gm_803DBF78` struct with inline `sprintf` format string literals in `fn_801A9FCC`.
Moved and annotated static data declarations, including Vec3 constants, BSS/SBSS variables, and `gm_803DBFB4`, using address comments.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: adds `/* 3B7D68 */ static const Vec3 gm_803B7D68`
raw_diff: moves gm_804D67D0 through gm_804D67F4 declarations near file top
raw_diff: changes `static int gm_803DBFB4[]` to `/* 3DBFB4 */ static int gm_803DBFB4[]`

what_changed.text: Added a `sdata2_order` helper referencing `82.0f` and `290.0f`, apparently to affect float ordering.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: adds `static void sdata2_order(void)` with `(void) 82.0f` and `(void) 290.0f`

what_changed.text: Added `#pragma force_active on` around `unused_floats` containing nine float values formerly held inside `gm_803DBF78`.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: adds `#pragma push`, `#pragma force_active on`, `static float unused_floats[]`, `#pragma pop`

what_changed.text: Updated splits so `gmopening.c` owns `.bss` range `0x80480B38..0x80480D58`, while `gm_1A4C.c` BSS ends at `0x80480B38`.

smart_moves.text: The PR avoided using a fake aggregate struct as a source-level access path for format strings once the symbol metadata separated floats from strings.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `fn_801A9FCC` uses inline format strings instead of offsets from `data`

smart_moves.text: Split metadata was adjusted together with source declarations, keeping BSS ownership visible in `splits.txt` rather than compensating only in C.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gmopening gains .bss range in splits.txt
raw_diff: gm_80480B38 BSS declaration appears in gmopening.c

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a code object appears to bundle floats and strings only because generated output exposed adjacent data, split the symbol metadata and keep ordinary string literals inline if the source code uses them directly.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: symbols split 0x803DBF78 float data from lbl_803DBF9C/lbl_803DBFA8 strings
raw_diff: `sprintf` calls use inline format strings

decomp_lessons.lesson: Linking a TU can require split/symbol metadata changes in addition to source changes; here BSS ownership and data symbol sizes/scopes were part of the attempt.
Author-reported objdiff text matching is not enough to mark a file Matching when data mismatch remains unresolved.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Everything matches in objdiff, but something causes a data mismatch if the file is marked as `Matching`.

naming_conventions.text: Address-style names such as `gm_803DBF78`, `lbl_803DBF9C`, `gm_80480B38`, and `gm_804D67D0` were preserved rather than replaced with speculative semantic names.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: symbols.txt uses gm_/lbl_ address-style names
raw_diff: gmopening.c declarations use address-style gm_ names

naming_conventions.text: Address comments were added to several declarations to make section placement explicit without changing the canonical symbol names.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Split one generated-looking struct into separate float storage and inline string literal uses, backed by symbols.txt labels.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: sprintf calls use inline strings
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Touches data ownership and literal placement; PR body says data mismatch still occurs if file is marked Matching.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use `#pragma force_active on` around `unused_floats` to retain otherwise unused float data.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Pragma-based retention is a narrow matching tactic that needs verifier and adjacent-regression evidence; the slice only includes the author's objdiff/data-mismatch note.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Add `sdata2_order` with unused float references to influence `.sdata2` ordering.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Ordering helper appears data-order motivated; no build/checkdiff details beyond author body are present.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Change split ownership so gmopening owns the BSS range containing `gm_80480B38`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Split boundary changes can affect neighboring TU data ownership; no review discussion is available in the slice.

evidence_refs: human_pr_text: author states objdiff matches but data mismatch blocks Matching marking
changed_files: 3 changed files with line counts
notes: The diff evidence is complete and supports changed-file and tactic extraction.
The only validation statement is the author's PR body; no build log, objdiff output, checkdiff output, or reviewer confirmation is present.
No review comments are available, so reviewer intent or accepted rationale cannot be inferred.
The PR was merged per metadata, but the slice still records the author's unresolved data mismatch note.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Do not keep a fake aggregate when symbol metadata separates floats from string literals
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2615, `gm_803DBF78` changed from a source struct containing floats and strings into a 0x24 local float object plus separate string labels, and `fn_801A9FCC` used inline format strings instead of pointer offsets into the aggregate.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Objdiff text match can still leave data mismatch unresolved
accepted_candidate_records.text: The PR body says everything matched in objdiff, but marking gmopening as Matching caused a data mismatch. Treat this as evidence that text closure and Matching status may need separate data-section verification.

source_update_candidates.title: gmopening split ownership includes BSS range after PR #2615
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2615 changed `config/GALE01/splits.txt` so `melee/gm/gmopening.c` owns `.bss start:0x80480B38 end:0x80480D58`, and shortened `melee/gm/gm_1A4C.c` BSS to end at `0x80480B38`.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential durable split-boundary/path fact; should be curator-checked against current source before promotion.

source_update_candidates.title: gmopening data metadata around 0x803DBF78 was split into float and string labels
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2615 changed `gm_803DBF78` at `.data:0x803DBF78` from size 0x3C global to size 0x24 local data:float and added string labels `lbl_803DBF9C` and `lbl_803DBFA8`, each size 0xC.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential symbol metadata fact; current symbols should be checked before promotion.

source_update_candidates.title: Pragma/data-order helpers need explicit verification when used for linking
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2615 used `#pragma force_active on` for `unused_floats` and a `sdata2_order` helper while the author still reported a data mismatch when marking the file Matching. This reinforces that pragma and data-order tactics should include narrow objdiff/checkdiff and data regression evidence.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: objdiff matches but Matching causes data mismatch
source_update_candidates.reason: Mostly already covered by existing standards, but this PR is a concrete example curator may want to cite.

rejection_notes: Do not record that gmopening was successfully marked `Matching`; the PR body explicitly says data mismatch occurred if marked Matching, and no status-file change is present in the slice.
Do not infer reviewer approval rationale; there were no review comments or reviews in the loaded PR slice.
Do not treat `sdata2_order` or `unused_floats` as preferred style; they are matching tactics with limited evidence in this slice.
Do not promote exact current path facts without checking current source, because this intake only proves the historical PR diff.
